rules.name: discount-cap-thirty
rules.reason: enforced by construction — the program rejects any deal that fails the condition before the next line runs

enforcement.sourceLine: enforce that deal's discount_percent is less than 30, or fail with error message: 'Discounts of 30% or more require VP approval — submit through the VP escalation queue, not the standard CRO desk'

compiledCheck.code: if (!(deal?.discount_percent < 30)) { return res.status(403).json({ error: "Discounts of 30% or more require VP approval — submit through the VP escalation queue, not the standard CRO desk", rule: "discount-cap-thirty" }); }

witness.reason: runtime witness skipped: the compiled application needs `jsonwebtoken` installed to boot. The math proof still stands; install dependencies and re-run the audit for runtime corroboration.

rules.name: price-floor-positive
rules.reason: enforced by construction — the program rejects any deal that fails the condition before the next line runs

enforcement.sourceLine: enforce that deal's list_price is greater than 0, or fail with error message: 'List price must be positive — zero or negative prices are never valid'

compiledCheck.code: if (!(deal?.list_price > 0)) { return res.status(403).json({ error: "List price must be positive — zero or negative prices are never valid", rule: "price-floor-positive" }); }

witness.reason: runtime witness skipped: the compiled application needs `jsonwebtoken` installed to boot. The math proof still stands; install dependencies and re-run the audit for runtime corroboration.

rules.name: discount-not-over-cap
rules.reason: enforced by construction — the program rejects any deal that fails the condition before the next line runs

enforcement.sourceLine: enforce that deal's discount_percent is less than 100, or fail with error message: 'Discount cannot meet or exceed 100% — that would mean giving the product away or paying the customer'

compiledCheck.code: if (!(deal?.discount_percent < 100)) { return res.status(403).json({ error: "Discount cannot meet or exceed 100% — that would mean giving the product away or paying the customer", rule: "discount-not-over-cap" }); }
